case_title: FUAD HALILOVIC VS. SIDNEY DE JESUS SOUZA SAMPAIO

docket_entries.description: THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF HALILOVIC, FUAD RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT SAMPAIO, SIDNEY DE JESUS SOUZA $2,827.95 PRINCIPAL, $50.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $2,877.95 STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED

SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL CALENDAR OF APR-10-2019 - SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT AFFIRMED. THE SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT OF JAN-29-2019 REMAINS THE SAME. THIS JUDGMENT ON APPEAL IS FINAL. EXHIBITS WILL BE DESTROYED PURSUANT TO CCP 1952.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT RESPONDENT HALILOVIC, FUAD RECOVER FROM APPELLANT SAMPAIO, SIDNEY DE JESUS SOUZA $2,827.95 PRINCIPAL, $50.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $2,877.95, JUDGEMNT TO BE OFFSET BY THE INSURANCE PROCEEDS PAID TO PLAINTIFF, WHICH PLAINTIFF TESTIFIED EXCEEDED THE AMOUNT OF THE JUDGMENT, THUS PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN FULLY COMPENSATED AND DEFENDNAT OWES NOTING MORE. calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Apr-10-2019 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sidney De Jesus Sampaio, Appellant, appearing on his own behlaf; accompanied by his witness Fooad Leghaei;
Faud Halilovic, Respondent, appearing on his own behalf;
No other appearance.

This cause comes on regularly for trial de novo. All partires and witnes are sworn in:

Faud Halilovic, Respondent/Plaintiff testifies on his own behalf.
Mr Faud state that Uber insurance stated that the driver Mr. Sampaio was covered by Uber Insurance. Respondent states that Uber Insurance has paid insurance claim after the intial Small Claims Judgement matter was decided in excess of the small Claims judgement awardered

The following documentary evidence is provided: (no new evidence provided at the time of todays hearing)

Sidney De Jesus Sampaio, Appellant testifies on his own behalf/behalf of Appellant/Defenedant.

Foad Leghaei, witness to appellants testiies on behalf of appellant.
States that he was not at the accident, But handles the insuruance for Infininty and Uber.

The following documentary evidence is provided;

Exhibit 1: Pictures of parked traffic and damage to repondents vehicles

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Stumpf; Clerk: W. Trupek, Not Reported (525/JPT)

PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM
Off Calendar Jan-29-2019 Judgment Entered
